title: Huma Qureshi joins Jantar Mantar protest with rumoured beau Rachit Singh, Saqib Saleem at 2 AM, marches with students
body: Here's the latest update from the Bollywood world:

Actors Huma Qureshi and Saqib Saleem joined a growing band of celebrities who have thrown their weight behind the ongoing student protests in Delhi and the rest of the country. They were accompanied by fellow star Rachit Singh. Late in the intervening night between Wednesday and Thursday, the star siblings and Rachit showed up at Jantar Mantar in the national capital and marched with the students to show solidarity with their protests. For over thousands of students, led by the Cockroach Janta Party , have been protesting in Delhi, demanding the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan over alleged irregularities in the education system.

Adding to this, Huma Qureshi and Saqib Saleem at Jantar Mantar. Huma and Saqib at Jantar Mantar On Thursday morning, vlogger Syed Umair Ali shared a video of Huma and Saqib walking with the protesters at Jantar Mantar. A text on the video shared it was shot at 2:13 AM. The video showed Huma, dressed in a black top and jeans, smiling and walking with the protesters. Rachit, dressed in a casual white shirt, walked beside her, occasionally taking her hand. Saqib could be seen walking alongside them. Rachit and Huma of late worked together in the movie Baby Do Die Do, which was produced by Huma and Saqib under their banner.
Meanwhile, View this post on Instagram A post shared by Syed Umair Ali (@syed_umair_ali_vlogs)

Notably, Tens of thousands of young protesters, led by the Cockroach Janta Party, are assembled at Jantar Mantar, a designated protest site in New Delhi, calling on Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan to step down. Protests have also sprung up in cities like Mumbai and Bengaluru. “This movement will not stop until we get the resignation of Pradhan,” the CJP put up a post on X. “We will fight together, we will win together.” Pradhan has struck a defiant tone in response to the protests, accusing the Congress party of using “students as political tools to manufacture disruption,” according to a post on X on Tuesday. He shared the government was committed to addressing students' concerns about the National Eligibility cum Entrance Test, one of the world’s largest medical admission exams, which triggered the recent protests.

As per the latest buzz, Earlier this week, the protests turned violent as public anger culminated in violent demonstrations in the capital when police used batons and tear gas to disperse crowds. On Tuesday, police forcibly removed senior opposition leaders from a sit-in outside Prime Minister Modi’s home.

In further updates, Abhimanyu Mathur is Deputy Editor, Entertainment at Hindustan Times. With almost 15 years of experience in writing about everything from films and TV shows to cricket matches and elections, he inhales and exhales pop culture and news. Currently, he watches movies and TV shows and talks to celebrities for a living, while occasionally writing about them as well. A journalism graduate of Delhi College of Arts and Commerce, Delhi University, Abhimanyu began his career with Hindustan Times at the age of 20, swapping classrooms for newsrooms at an early age. He began his journey in the early days of digital journalism, later switching to the madness of print journalism. Work has led him to far off places like Japan and Jordan, as well as to the interiors of Haryana and the Indo-Pak border. He dabbled in city reporting in places like Meerut, Gurgaon, and Delhi, covered the Olympics and Cricket World Cups, before finding his calling in entertainment and lifestyle during the pandemic. A Rotten Tomatoes Certified Movie Critic, he is equally at home covering stories on ground as he is interviewing celebrities and studios, and sometimes prefers to shepherd teams in delivering traffic through the day. Even as his role has evolved from reporter to supervisor over the years, his first love remains writing (and of late, talking on camera). With a good understanding of cinema and its trends, and a keen eye for detail, he continues to spark conversations around showbiz for readers around the world. Read More

title: Aamir Khan's 3 Idiots re-releasing in theatres amid Sonam Wangchuk row? Producer Vidhu Vinod Chopra says ‘false news’
body: Here's the latest update from the Bollywood world:

Days after media reports claimed that Rajkumar Hirani's 3 Idiots would be re-rolled out in theatres, the movie's producer, Vidhu Vinod Chopra, has denied the reports, calling them ‘factually incorrect’. The news of the re-rollout came days after the movie was embroiled in a minor row when Aamir Khan denied that the movie was inspired by educator and activist Sonam Wangchuk .

Adding to this, Directed by Rajkumari Hirani, 3 Idiots starred Aamir Khan, Sharman Joshi and R Madhavan. 3 Idiots not re-releasing Last week, media reports claimed that 3 Idiots would be re-rolled out in theatres on September 4. On Wednesday, Vidhu Vinod Chopra shared an announcement through his production house, Vidhu Vinod Chopra Films. The statement read: “With reference to recent media reports claiming that 3 Idiots will be re-rolled out on 4th September, Vidhu Vinod Chopra Films would like to clarify that these reports are false and factually incorrect.”

Meanwhile, The filmmaker also urged followers and the media to rely only on "verified channels" for such announcements. “We request members of the media and followers to rely solely on official announcements made through Vidhu Vinod Chopra Films' verified channels. We urge media publications to refrain from publishing or circulating unverified information,” he added.

Notably, 3 Idiots is a Hindi movie written, edited and directed by Rajkumar Hirani and produced by Vidhu Vinod Chopra. The movie starred Aamir Khan, R. Madhavan and Sharman Joshi in the title roles, alongside Kareena Kapoor, Boman Irani, Mona Singh and Omi Vaidya in supporting roles. The movie was the highest-grossing Indian movie at the time of its rollout in 2009, and has remained an influential movie since. According to media reports, a sequel to the movie is currently in the making.

As per the latest buzz, The movie presents a satirical drama about India's education system through the story of three students at an Indian engineering college, exposing the intrinsic paternalism within it. It was shared to be partly inspired by Chetan Bhagat's Five Point Someone, and news reports at the time also highlighted that Aamir's character was inspired by Ladakhi educator and inventor Sonam Wangchuk. The movie's climax was shot at Wangchuk's school in Ladakh.

In further updates, However, after Wangchuk sat on a hunger strike demanding the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan over irregularities in the NEET exam, Aamir Khan denied any connection between 3 Idiots and him, saying the movie was not based on him.

On top of that, Abhimanyu Mathur is Deputy Editor, Entertainment at Hindustan Times. With almost 15 years of experience in writing about everything from films and TV shows to cricket matches and elections, he inhales and exhales pop culture and news. Currently, he watches movies and TV shows and talks to celebrities for a living, while occasionally writing about them as well. A journalism graduate of Delhi College of Arts and Commerce, Delhi University, Abhimanyu began his career with Hindustan Times at the age of 20, swapping classrooms for newsrooms at an early age. He began his journey in the early days of digital journalism, later switching to the madness of print journalism. Work has led him to far off places like Japan and Jordan, as well as to the interiors of Haryana and the Indo-Pak border. He dabbled in city reporting in places like Meerut, Gurgaon, and Delhi, covered the Olympics and Cricket World Cups, before finding his calling in entertainment and lifestyle during the pandemic. A Rotten Tomatoes Certified Movie Critic, he is equally at home covering stories on ground as he is interviewing celebrities and studios, and sometimes prefers to shepherd teams in delivering traffic through the day. Even as his role has evolved from reporter to supervisor over the years, his first love remains writing (and of late, talking on camera). With a good understanding of cinema and its trends, and a keen eye for detail, he continues to spark conversations around showbiz for readers around the world. Read More

title: Govt increases petrol price by Rs6.39, diesel by Rs7.83 per litre
body: Here's what's trending across India right now:

comments Join our Whatsapp Channel Add Dawn as a trusted source The government on Wednesday increased the price of petrol by Rs6.39 and that of high-speed diesel (HSD) by Rs7.83 per litre to pass on the impact of fluctuating global prices following renewed regional hostilities in the Persian Gulf.
Adding to this, Following the changes, the price of petrol stands at Rs327.12 per litre and that of HSD at Rs375.04.
Meanwhile, The Petroleum Division’s notification shared the new prices would be applicable on July 23 (Thursday).
Notably, Earlier today, the All Pakistan Petroleum Pump Owners Association (APPPOA) deferred its nationwide strike call for two weeks after negotiations with the petroleum minister.
As per the latest buzz, A day earlier, the association revealed a nationwide strike after negotiations with the government over daily petroleum pricing failed.
In further updates, The diesel price has come down from a peak of Rs520.35 recorded on April 3. Its price had started rising from Rs281 per litre after the US-Iran war broke out on February 28.
On top of that, The petrol price had peaked at Rs458.41 on April 3 after beginning its upward trajectory from Rs266 in the first week of March.

Earlier, Petroleum Minister Ali Pervaiz Malik opened up that fuel prices would now be fixed on a daily basis due to fluctuations in international market prices following renewed hostilities between Iran and the US.

title: ગુજરાત વરસાદમાં બે ભાગમાં વહેંચાયું : દક્ષિણમાં જળબંબાકાર પણ બાકીના વિસ્તારોમાં વરસાદ કેમ નહીં?
body: Here's what's trending across India right now:

BBC News, ગુજરાતી કન્ટેન્ટ પર જાવ સમાચાર વીડિયો ઑડિયો વિભાગો સમાચાર ગુજરાત ભારત વિદેશ હવામાન સમાચાર સ્પોર્ટ્સ મૅગેઝિન લોકપ્રિય ગુજરાત ભારત વિદેશ હવામાન સમાચાર સ્પોર્ટ્સ મૅગેઝિન લોકપ્રિય સુરત, નવસાર���, વલસાડ સહિત દક્ષિણ ગુજરાત જળબંબાકાર, બીજી તરફ અડધા ગુજરાતમાં વરસાદની ચિંતાજનક ઘટ ઇમેજ સ્રોત, NIRAV KANSARA/BBC
Adding to this, દક્ષિણ ગુજરાતમાં છેલ્લા ચાર દિવસથી ભારે વરસાદ પડી રહ્યો છે અને હજુ પણ એક અઠવાડિયા સુધી મોટા ભાગના જિલ્લાઓમાં ભારેથી અતિભારે વરસાદ પડવાની આગાહી છે.

Meanwhile, એક તરફ સૌરાષ્ટ્ર-કચ્છ અને ઉત્તર ગુજરાતમાં આ વખતે વરસાદની ચિંતાજનક ઘટ છે, વાવેતરને અસર થઈ છે અને ઘાસચારાના અભાવે પશુપાલન મુશ્કેલ બની ગયું છે, ત્યારે સુરત, વલસાડ, નવસારી, તાપી સહિતના જિલ્લાઓમાં સતત વરસાદ પડી રહ્યો છે.
Notably, મંગળવારની સવારના 6 વાગ્યાથી બુધવારે સવારના 6 વાગ્યા સુધીના ચોવીસ કલાકમાં વલસાડના કપરાડા તાલુકામાં 15.63 ઇંચ વરસાદ નોંધાયો હતો જે સમગ્ર ગુજરાતમાં સૌથી વધારે છે. આજે પણ અહીં વરસાદ ચાલુ છે.
As per the latest buzz, ત્યાર પછી વલસાડના જ નાનાપોંઢામાં 11 ઇંચ, નવસારીના ચિખલીમાં 9.61 ઇંચ, વલસાડના પારડીમાં 9.37 ઇંચ, નવસારીના વાંસદામાં 9.25 ઇંચ વરસાદ પડ્યો છે.

In further updates, વલસાડ શહેરમાં 8.31 ઇંચ, ખેરગામમાં આઠ ઇંચ, વાપીમાં 7.28 ઇંચ, ડાંગના વઘઈમાં 7.20 ઇંચ અને વલસાડના ધરમપુરમાં 6.77 ઇંચ વરસાદ નોંધાયો હતો. જ્યારે સુરતના બારડોલીમાં ચોવીસ કલાકમાં 5.39 ઇંચ વરસાદ પડ્યો છે.
On top of that, સુરત શહેરમાં પણ ત્રણ દિવસથી વરસાદ ચાલુ છે અને નીચાણવાળા વિસ્તારોમાં ઘરોમાં પાણી ભરાઈ ગયા છે. એક પખવાડિયા અગાઉ સુરતમાં ભારે વરસાદ પડ્યો ત્યારે લગભગ 40થી વધુ લોકોનાં મોત થયાં હતાં.

નવસારી જિલ્લામાં સતત ચોથા દિવસે વરસાદી માહોલ છે. બીબીસી ગુજરાતીના નવસારીના સહયોગી સ્નેહલ પટેલે જણાવ્યા પ્રમાણે નવસારીના તમામ છ તાલુકામાં ભારે વરસાદ પડ્યો છે. જેના કારણે અંબિકા અને કાવેરી નદીમાં પાણી ભયજનક સ્તરે પહોંચ્યું છે. આ બંને નદીમાં પૂરની સ્થિતિના કારણે બિલીમોરા શહેરમાં લોકોને હાલાકી પડી રહી છે. ભારે વરસાદના કારણે જિલ્લાની તમામ શાળાઓમાં આજે રજા જાહેર કરવામાં આવી છે.

title: એક જ કચેરી પર શહેર અને 45 ગામડાનો વહીવટનો ભાર‎: જાફરાબાદ ખાતે પીજીવીસીએલની રૂરલ અને સિટી કચેરી અલગ કરો
body: Here's what's trending across India right now:

હોમ વીડિયો સર્ચ ઈ-પેપર ટૉપ ન્યૂઝ મારું શહેર મારું ગુજરાત ભાસ્કર ખાસ ઓરિજિનલ ક્રિકેટ એન્ટરટેઇનમેન્ટ ઈન્ડિયા ધર્મ દર્શન સ્પોર્ટ્સ વર્લ્ડ યુટિલિટી લાઇફસ્ટાઇલ બિઝનેસ રાશિફળ મેગેઝિન Gujarati News Local Gujarat Amreli Rajula The Burden Of Administration Of A City And 45 Villages On A Single Office એક જ કચેરી પર શહેર અને 45 ગામડાનો વહીવટનો ભાર‎: જાફરાબાદ ખાતે પીજીવીસીએલની રૂરલ અને સિટી કચેરી અલગ કરો રાજુલા 2 કલાક પેહલા કૉપી લિંક જાફરાબાદ ખાતે પશ્ચિમ ગુજરાત વીજ કંપની (PGVCL)ની રૂરલ અને સિટી કચેરી અલગ કરવાની માંગ ઉઠી છે. હાલમાં જાફરાબાદ શહેર તેમજ 45 ગામોના વીજ પુરવઠા અને વહીવટની કામગીરી એક જ કચેરી દ્વારા સંચાલિત કરવામાં આવી રહી છે, જેના કારણે સ્ટાફ પર કામનું ભારણ વધી રહ્યું છે
Adding to this, કચેરીમાં માત્ર 20 કર્મચારીના સ્ટાફથી સમગ્ર વિસ્તારનો વહીવટ કરવામાં આવતો હોવાથી લાઇનમેનની અછત સહિત વિવિધ સમસ્યાઓ સર્જાઈ રહી છે.

Meanwhile, વીજ પુરવઠામાં ખામી સર્જાય ત્યારે સમયસર કામગીરી કરવામાં મુશ્કેલી પડતી હોવાની ફરિયાદો સામે આવી રહી છે. સ્થાનિક લોકોનું કહેવું છે કે જાફરાબાદ શહેર અને ગ્રામ્ય વિસ્તારનો સતત વિકાસ થઈ રહ્યો છે તેમજ વીજ ગ્રાહકોની સંખ્યા પણ વધી રહી છે.

Notably, છતાં સ્ટાફ અને વહીવટી વ્યવસ્થામાં જરૂરી વધારો કરાયો નથી. આ મુદ્દે અમરેલી જિલ્લા ભાજપ મહામંત્રી ચેતનભાઈ શિયાળે ઊર્જામંત્રી કૌશિકભાઈ વેકરિયા તેમજ રાજુલા-જાફરાબાદના ધારાસભ્ય હીરાભાઈ સોલંકી સમક્ષ રજૂઆત કરી છે.
As per the latest buzz, રજૂઆતમાં જાફરાબાદ ખાતે રૂરલ અને સિટી માટે અલગ-અલગ કચેરી શરૂ કરવા, સ્ટાફ અને લાઇનમેનની સંખ્યા વધારવા તેમજ વીજસેવા વધુ કાર્યક્ષમ બનાવવા જરૂરી કાર્યવાહી માંગ કરાઈ છે.
In further updates, This website follows the DNPA Code of Ethics .

title: Bollywood blast master keeps it real with fire and fury
body: Here's the latest update from the Bollywood world:
From fireballs to gunfights, Bollywood special effects master Vishal Tyagi is going the old-fashioned way to create cinematic chaos in a way he says artificial intelligence simply cannot.

Adding to this, Bollywood blast master keeps it real with fire and fury The tanker explosion climax in his latest smash hit action movie "Dhurandhar 2" used 500 litres of petrol along with explosives in a carefully orchestrated stunt involving star Ranveer Singh.
Meanwhile, "The fragments and fires were 100 percent real," Tyagi, 48, told AFP in an interview.
Notably, The sequence reflects a growing trend among Indian filmmakers to deploy special effects, looking to deliver the type of authenticity that computer-generated imagery often cannot replicate.
As per the latest buzz, To create the explosion, Tyagi's team used a real train as a base, constructing the tanker with acrylic panels mounted on a metal frame.
In further updates, "Because acrylic melts into the air, that's why we see fragments flying," Tyagi shared.
On top of that, "Sometimes, to give a real look, we have to use something artificial."

Also an arms specialist, the shelves at Tyagi's warehouse in a Mumbai suburb are stacked with AK-47s, sniper rifles and rocket launchers, which he supplies as props for movie sets in India and abroad.

title: Bangladesh pin hopes on sporting diplomacy to host India in September
body: Here's what's trending across India right now:

Aminul Haque, Bangladesh’s State Minister for Youth and Sports, shared on Tuesday that the country was looking forward to hosting India in September and believes any distance between the two countries can be bridged through sporting diplomacy.

Adding to this, The BCB had revealed at the start of the year that they would host India for a three-match ODI and T20I series in August-September 2026, after the tour which was initially scheduled for 2025 was called off owing to diplomatic tensions between the two countries.

Meanwhile, Bangladesh and India have had a strained relationship in the recent past that forced the then Yunus-led government to withdraw their participation in the last T20 World Cup in India citing security issues, casting a doubt over the possibility of the tour.

Notably, With the BCB putting up television and media rights on sale earlier this month for the India series, the signs pointed towards the tour getting back on track. The notion swelled further after the schedule for India’s tour of Sri Lanka in August did not include T20Is as opposed to earlier discussions, potentially paving the way for India to then tour Bangladesh.
As per the latest buzz, The BCB also insisted that they will enhance security measures for the India series if required.

In further updates, However, fresh doubts emerged over the series after the BCB put the media rights sale process on hold despite issuing a tender notification inviting bids, as first reported by Cricbuzz on July 1.

On top of that, “The current government is giving utmost importance to sports diplomacy. And since cricket is a place of prestige for us, since cricket has a very strong position in our world cricket…we have already spoken to the Indian Embassy, they have also spoken to us and they are all waiting for India to come to Bangladesh in September,” Aminul told reporters at Dhaka on Tuesday.

“After arriving, I think that we will overcome the tension that was there (between the two countries) over a small incident in the past and hopefully our sports diplomacy, our communication, our movement, everything will continue with our neighbouring country,” he added.

title: महाराष्ट्रात मान्सूनचा जोर वाढला; जाणून घ्या तुमच्या जिल्ह्यातील हवामानाचा अंदाज
body: Here's what's trending across India right now:

BBC News, मराठी थेट मजकुरावर जा बातम्या पाहा विभाग बातम्या महाराष्ट्र भारत आंतरराष्ट्रीय हवामान आरोग्य सोपी गोष्ट लोकप्रिय महाराष्ट्र भारत आंतरराष्ट्रीय हवामान आरोग्य सोपी गोष्ट लोकप्रिय महाराष्ट्रात मान्सूनचा जोर वाढला; जाणून घ्या तुमच्या जिल्ह्यातील हवामानाचा अंदाज फोटो स्रोत, Getty Images

Adding to this, Role, बीबीसी मराठी प्रतिनिधी Published 22 जुलै 2026, 17:41 IST अपडेटेड 9 तासांपूर्वी वाचन वेळ: 3 मिनिटे महाराष्ट्रात गेल्या चोवीस तासांत अनेक ठिकाणी जोरदार पावसानं हजेरी लावली असून पुढचे काही दिवस राज्यात पावसाचा जोर कायम राहण्याची शक्यता आहे. कोकण, विदर्भ, मराठवाडा आणि मध्य महाराष्ट्रात पुढच्या चोवीस त��सांत हवामान कसं असेल, कुठल्या जिल्ह्यांत रेड अलर्ट दिला आहे जाणून घेऊया.

Meanwhile, पुढे दाखवलेला नकाशा 1 जूनपासून ते 22 जुलैपर्यंतची पावसाची स्थिती दाखवतो. हिरव्या जिल्ह्यांत सरासरीएवढा तर निळ्या रंगातील जिल्ह्यांत सरासरीपेक्षा जास्त पाऊस झाला आहे. पण विदर्भ आणि मराठवाड्यात बहुतांश जिल्ह्यांमध्ये पावसात मोठी तूट आहे. तर नंदुरबारमध्ये सर्वात कमी पाऊस झाला आहे.
Notably, पुढच्या चोवीस तासांत यात बदल घडू शकतो, कारण सध्या महाराष्ट्रातही मान्सून अगदी सक्रीय झाला आहे. 22 जुलैच्या दुपारच्या सॅटेलाईट दृश्यांमध्ये राज्यात अनेक ठिकाणी पावसाचे ढग दाटलेले दिसता.
As per the latest buzz, भारत फोरकास्टिंग मॉडेलनुसार मध्य प्रदेशावर एक हवेच्या वरच्या स्तरात सायक्लॉनिक सर्क्युलेशन म्हणजे चक्राकार वाऱ्यांची स्थिती तयार झाली आहे.
In further updates, परिणामी महाराष्ट्राच्या उत्तरेकडे गुजरातपासून जोरदार वारे पश्चिमेकडून पूर्वेकडे वाहतायत, ज्यानं राज्यात मान्सूनच्या पावसाला बळ मिळालं आहे.
On top of that, या वाऱ्यांमुळेच दक्षिण गुजरात तसंच महाराष्ट्राच्या किनारी भागांत आणि उत्तरेकडील भागात जोरदार पावसाची शक्यता आहे.
बीबीसी वेदर फोरकास्टनुसारही पुढच्या 24 तासांत, पालघर, ठाणे, मुंबई, रायगड, पुणे आणि नाशिकच्या घाट भागांत जोरदार ते अतीजोरदार पावसाची शक्यता आहे.

title: Social media addiction lawsuit against Meta is dropped
body: Here's what's trending across India right now:

Social media addiction lawsuit against Meta is dropped Sarah Perez 1:36 PM PDT · July 22, 2026 A day after Snap tentatively settled with the plaintiff in a social media addiction lawsuit, leaving Meta as the only remaining defendant, the case has been dropped. In a statement, Meta shared the plaintiff chose to drop his case against Meta without receiving any payment.
Adding to this, TikTok and Google’s YouTube had previously reached settlement agreements with the plaintiff. (Snap on Tuesday verified a tentative agreement had been reached.)
Meanwhile, The bellwether jury trial had been set to begin next week in the Superior Court of California in Los Angeles.

Notably, The plaintiff, a Florida teenager known by the initials “R.K.C.,” had sued the social media companies for creating addictive platforms. It was one of thousands of similar lawsuits from teens, schools, and state attorneys general that had accused the big tech companies of knowingly creating addictive platforms.

As per the latest buzz, The precedent that would have been set by this lawsuit and others could have impacted how the companies build their apps, known for features that keep people engaged, like the infinite scroll and their continual buzz of notifications.

In further updates, The plaintiff’s decision to drop the case follows Meta’s loss in a New Mexico case earlier this year , which marked its first courtroom defeat over social media harms. Meta was ordered to pay $375 million in penalties after the company was found to have misled consumers about the safety of its platforms and endangered children.
On top of that, In March, a Los Angeles jury also handed both Meta and Google another defeat , awarding the defendant in that case some $6 million in damages.

Meta had been prepared to argue that the plaintiff in this case had allegedly only used Facebook and Instagram accounts for minutes per day on average, and was planning to claim that most of his accounts had been created after hiring a lawyer.

title: Mumbai schools closed: Are schools shut on Thursday, July 23, due to heavy rains? BMC shares update
body: Here's what's trending across India right now:

Sharing an update on whether schools are closed in Mumbai or not due to heavy rains, the Brihanmumbai Municipal Corporation (BMC) maintained that all institutions across Mumbai will remain functional today, Wednesday, and even tomorrow, Thursday.

Adding to this, In Mumbai, the BMC typically declares a public holiday across educational institutions when the IMD declares a ‘red’ warning for the city. owing to the possibility of intense spells of rainfall for a prolonged period.
Meanwhile, An IMD red alert, for perspective, is sounded when the total rainfall in a span of 24 hours exceeds 204 mm rain.
Notably, However, the city is unlikely to witness such heavy spells this week with the India Meteorological Department issuing a yellow alert on Wednesday and Thursday.

As per the latest buzz, While the weather bureau’s yellow alert calls for alertness amongst administration and citizens, school closure is unlikely unless the IMD upgrades its forecast warning to red warning or an orange alert when there is possibility of heavy downpour with gusty winds.
In further updates, According to the IMD forecast, Mumbai will remain under a yellow alert until Friday, July 24, as it has been for the third day today, seeing heavy downpour.

On top of that, Thane, Palghar, Raigad and Ratnagiri have been expected to experience severe weather conditions, with IMD forecast predicting a very heavy rainfall in the regions. The IMD has sounded an orange alert in the region for the next 24 hours.

The IMD Santacruz observatory recorded 48.4 mm of rainfall, while Colaba recorded 35.6 mm. The BMC’s automatic weather system showed the western suburbs at 64.91 mm, eastern suburbs at 48.7 mm, and the island city at 36.65 mm.

title: Teenager drops social media addiction lawsuit against Meta
body: Here's what's trending across India right now:

The 15-year-old plaintiff, known by the initials R.K.C., had in recent weeks settled the same claims against TikTok, Snapchat, and YouTube, all of which have been accused in a large lawsuit of creating addictive platforms harmful to children.
Adding to this, R.K.C.'s claims were to go before a Los Angeles jury next week as part of court proceedings that have seen Meta and YouTube lose once already .
Meanwhile, A Meta spokeswoman told the BBC: "The claims never held up, and this outcome makes clear that we will not back away from defending ourselves against baseless lawsuits."

Notably, Lawyers for R.K.C., Emily Jeffcott and Rahul Ravipudi, told the BBC that the young plaintiff "came into this process wanting to hold social media companies accountable and push for changes to protect young people like himself".

As per the latest buzz, The lawyers noted concerns about a jury trial stretching on for weeks and shared R.K.C. was "ready to close this chapter and focus on his recovery and engage in therapy as he aspires to have a normal life".
In further updates, The Meta spokeswoman shared he received no payment for dropping his claims.

On top of that, R.K.C. had claimed that common social media features such as infinite scroll and autoplay, both of which continuously and automatically show users new content on a platform, drove compulsive use that became a type of addiction.
He argued it caused him anxiety and sleep deprivation, among other issues.

title: 'Cockroaches never die': Why RAF officer Sonia Sehrawat, her Instagram story are at the centre of CJP protest | India News
body: Here's what's trending across India right now:

Amid the ongoing Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) protest in Delhi, a name has surfaced online - Rapid Action Force (RAF) officer Sonia Sehrawat, who was present at Jantar Mantar on the day of the Parliament march on July 20.

Adding to this, Rapid Action Force (RAF) officer Sonia Sehrawat; now-deleted Instagram story (X/@MahuaMoitra) She found herself at the centre of a row over an Instagram story she purportedly posted a day after the march. A screenshot of that story, which appears to insult the protesters, has since gone widely shared.
Meanwhile, In what reports say is a now-deleted post, Sehrawat had posted a short reel on her Instagram story that read, “Can’t fix themselves and they wanna fix the country.”
Notably, The alleged post sparked sharp criticism from CJP, which accused Sehrawat of mocking the protesters and justifying what it described as the "brutality" of the security forces.

As per the latest buzz, Calling it "absolutely shocking," the CJP shared in a post on X, "This RAF official actually sees the peaceful protesters as cockroaches and thinks that the brutality of the police was justified because of that. But you see, we are cockroaches. And cockroaches never die."

In further updates, The post also invited criticism from TMC MP Mahua Moitra, who shared the screenshot of the story on X, saying, “RAF assistant commandant Sonia Shehrawat’s insta profile calling our children protestors cockroaches. Let’s find out who she is, where she lives, what’s her record? Let’s name & shame & drag each of these zealots through the law.”

On top of that, On July 20, the day of the CJP's 'Sansad Chalo' march , party founder Abhijeet Dipke publicly questioned Sehrawat over allegations that RAF personnel had used excessive force against protesters, including women.
Standing atop a lorry, Dipke addressed the officer directly, saying, "Ma'am, as a woman, I want to know... Do you support brutality against women being done by men?"

title: Dhanbad News: पाइपलाइन में लीकेज और बिजली कटौती से झरिया की जलापूर्ति ठप
body: Here's what's trending across India right now:

फोटो वीडियो शहर चुनें ई- पेपर साइन इन होम राज्य देश क्रिकेट पंडित जी से चैट लाइव स्कोर मनोरंजन बिजनेस करियर विदेश Loan- 5Lac धर्म काम की बात ऑटो राशिफल सावन 2026 एक्सप्लेनर्स लाइफस्टाइल ट्रेंडिंग खबरें चुनाव वीडियो आपके लिए खास वायरल ओपिनियन फोटो क्राइम Dhanbad News: पाइपलाइन में लीकेज और बिजली कटौती से झरिया की जलापूर्ति ठप Jul 23, 2026 01:05 am IST By Newswrap हिन्दुस्तान , धनबाद Dhanbad News: झमाडा के जामाडोबा जल संयंत्र से झरिया जलमीनार को आने वाली मुख्य पाइपलाइन में एक बार फिर बड़ा लीकेज हुआ। इससे पानी की सप्लाई पूरी तरह रुक गई और तीन लाख लोगों को संकट का सामना करना पड़ा। विभाग ने युद्धस्तर पर मरम्मत कार्य शुरू किया, लेकिन बार-बार की बिजली कटौती ने जल आपूर्ति में बाधा डाल दी। Dhanbad News: झमाडा के जामाडोबा जल संयंत्र से झरिया जलमीनार को आने वाली मुख्य पाइपलाइन में बड़की टांड़ के पास मंगलवार को एक बार फिर बड़ा लीकेज हो गया। पाइप फटने के कारण झरिया जलमीनार को होने वाली पानी की सप्लाई पूरी तरह रुक गई। इससे बुधवार को पूरे झरिया क्षेत्र में जलापूर्ति ठप रही और करीब तीन लाख की आबादी को पानी के संकट से जूझना पड़ा। बरसात के मौसम में भी पानी की किल्लत झेल रहे लोगों में भारी रोष देखा जा रहा है। पाइपलाइन में खराबी की सूचना मिलते ही विभाग की ओर से युद्धस्तर पर मरम्मत कार्य शुरू किया गया। लीकेज को ठीक कर लिया गया। हालांकि, लीकेज सुधरने के बाद भी उपभोक्ताओं की परेशानी कम नहीं हुई।

Adding to this, बुधवार को जामाडोबा जल संयंत्र में बिजली कटौती का सामना जल संयंत्र को करना पड़ा। सुबह 6:00 बजे से लेकर शाम 5:00 बजे तक करीब 11 घंटों के भीतर पांच बार बिजली कटी। आधा घंटा से लेकर डेढ़ घंटा तक की बार-बार की कटौती के कारण जल संयंत्र में 5 घंटे से अधिक समय तक पानी का भंडारण पूरी तरह बाधित रहा। देर शाम बिजली व्यवस्था बहाल होने के बाद जल संयंत्र से झरिया जल मीनार को पानी की सप्लाई दोबारा शुरू की गई है। जल संयंत्र के कनीय अभियंता आशुतोष राणा ने बताया कि पाइपलाइन का लीकेज पूरी तरह ठीक कर लिया गया है, लेकिन बार-बार होने वाली बिजली कटौती ने काम में बड़ी बाधा खड़ी की है। यदि रात में बिजली की आपूर्ति नियमित और सही ढंग से बनी रहती है, तो झरिया जलमीनार में पर्याप्त पानी जमा होने के बाद गुरुवार की सुबह झरिया शहरी क्षेत्र और उसके आस-पास के अन्य सभी इलाकों में सामान्य रूप से जलापूर्ति शुरू कर दी जाएगी।

title: Dhurandhar fame Ayesha Khan detained by Mumbai Police during student protest, shares video asking, “Just a simple why?”
body: Here's the latest update from the Bollywood world:

According to Ayesha, she reached Dadar around 4 pm to extend her support to the ongoing student protests that have spread to Mumbai. She shared that before she could raise any slogans or begin demonstrating, a police officer informed her that protests were not permitted at the location. When she asked, “Bata dijiye dusra aur kya raasta hain,” she claimed the situation escalated quickly.

Adding to this, In a video statement shared on Instagram, Ayesha alleged that around 15 police personnel, including both male and female officers, surrounded her group and instructed them to board a police van. She further claimed that when they resisted, they were forcibly taken into the vehicle.
Meanwhile, A post shared by Ayesha Khan (@ayeshaakhan_official)

Notably, Describing the incident, Ayesha shared, “I was pulled by 4-5 women. I used all my strength to try not getting into the van but they pulled us in. Poorey van mein hum poochte rahein ki aap ne humein kyu detain kiya hain? Humne galti kya kiya hain? No answer, no accountability, nothing. Kisi ne koi jawab nahi diya aur humein Dadar se door Worli lekar aaye.”

As per the latest buzz, She added that while officers at the Worli police station treated them respectfully, she still did not receive an explanation for the detention. According to Ayesha, her primary question remains why she and others were detained before they had even begun protesting.
In further updates, A post shared by Ayesha Khan (@ayeshaakhan_official)

On top of that, In another Instagram post, Ayesha shared a video that appeared to show her being pushed into a police van during the incident. Alongside the clip, she wrote, “Dragged, detained for standing in peace. Asked questions to all the police people present there on why were we being detained? But no answer whatsoever! Sadak pe khade rehna kabse crime ho Gaya? Today will be the most peaceful sleep of my life. I stood for what was right! Still in the police station asking the same question! Why am I detained? Which law did I break? Forget about one’s democratic right, and protecting it, instead you are harming your own citizens, for what? I see shame in some of their eyes, but they are bound by their duties, they know, we know, what’s right and what’s wrong. My stand is clear. We lack accountability, EVERYWHERE. I still have hope, we will see a better future.”

The demonstrations in Mumbai are part of a wider wave of student protests that began in New Delhi and have now spread to multiple cities across the country. Protesters have been demanding accountability and have also called for the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan. Demonstrations have taken place at several locations in Mumbai, including Shivaji Park and Chaityabhoomi, with some protesters claiming they were detained even before reaching the protest sites.

title: Paper leak serious issue, my heart goes out to those hurt: Salman Khan
body: Here's the latest update from the Bollywood world:
New Delhi, Calling paper leak a serious issue, Salman Khan on Wednesday came out in support of protesting students and shared his heart goes out to those who were hurt while demonstrating peacefully.

Adding to this, Paper leak serious issue, my heart goes out to those hurt: Salman Khan The Bollywood star appreciated the stand taken by the students who, he shared, have shown dedication, sincerity, and keenness to study hard for their own futures, and for a greater, educated India.

Meanwhile, "It was such a peaceful movement, feel so sad that it had to take a violent turn. My heart goes out to the students and their families who were hurt. Paper leak is a very serious issue, and I am glad to know and see that the kids of our country have come together for a better educational system, and their parents supported them," the star wrote in a carefully-worded post on X.
Notably, He, however, shared the protests should not be "hijacked politically".

As per the latest buzz, "This andolan, this protest, is the right way to go about it, and the students have done this peacefully. So courageous and brave. Driven and motivated towards education, this generation will make India proud.

In further updates, "This issue is between the students and the educational system, it should not be hijacked politically, the credit should only go to the students of our country, and I am sure the government will also give them all the support and make a stronger educational system. It's a win-win situation. Hoping and praying for a positive decision," he wrote.
On top of that, The star shared education should get trendier and more fashionable so that India becomes an education hub.

The student protests over the alleged NEET paper leak have found support from many in the movie industry, including Naseeruddin Shah, Ratna Pathak Shah, Shabana Azmi, Prakash Raj, Rajkummar Rao, Revathy, Tovino Thomas and Anurag Kashyap.

title: Salman Khan breaks silence on CJP protests with his own picture as a student: ‘It was such a peaceful movement’
body: Here's the latest update from the Bollywood world:

Over the last few days, several Bollywood celebrities have openly spoken up about the CJP-led student protest. On Monday, after students marched from Jantar Mantar to Parliament and were met with police force, including lathi-charge and tear gas, many celebrities took to social media to condemn the use of force against the protesters. On Wednesday, star Salman Khan spoke on the protests.

Adding to this, Salman Khan shared a picture of himself to pen the statement. ‘My heart goes out to the students’ He posted on Instagram and wrote a long note. It read, “It was such a peaceful movement, feel so sad that it had to take a violent turn. My heart goes out to the students and their families who were hurt. Paper leak is a very serious issue, and I am glad to know n see that the kids of our country have come together for a better educational system, and their parents supported them.”

Meanwhile, He added, “I truly appreciate the stand they have taken, showing dedication, sincerity, and keenness to study hard n make their future and a greater, educated India. This andolan, this protest, is the right way to go abt it, n the students have done this peacefully. So courageous and brave. Driven and motivated towards education, this generation will make India proud.”
Notably, View this post on Instagram A post shared by Salman Khan (@beingsalmankhan)

As per the latest buzz, He added, “This issue is between the students and the educational system, it should not be hijacked politically, the credit should only go to the students of our country, and I am sure the government will also give them all the support n make it a stronger educational system. It’s a win-win situation. Hoping n praying for a positive decision. God bless all of you who wanna be educated.”

In further updates, "Education should be the next trend and fashion, and should get trendier n more fashionable yr by yr, itna k bahar se log (people from outside) come to India to study and India becomes an educational hub," he concluded.

On top of that, According to Delhi officials, during Monday’s protest by Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) in the New Delhi area, the protesters displayed unruly, aggressive, and violent behaviour. Despite repeated warnings and lawful directions issued by the police, they refused to disperse and deliberately violated the prohibitory orders in force. The protestors, however, claim that Delhi Police personnel targeted them indiscriminately, leading to several injuries on their side as well. Several videos on social media show Delhi Police personnel lathi-charging the students and resorting to firing tear gas. Protestors have alleged that people in civil clothes without police accreditation attacked them as well.

The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) called for a march from Jantar Mantar, where the protestors have been camped for a month, to the Parliament. The protests have been ongoing amid allegations of irregularities in the education sector, particularly the alleged leaks of NEET exams. Social activist Sonam Wangchuk also joined the protest earlier this month, starting a hunger strike at Jantar Mantar. The protestors met representatives of the central government on Tuesday, but the protests continue.

title: Imran Khan joins CJP protest in Mumbai, marches with students and clicks pictures with them. Watch
body: Here's the latest update from the Bollywood world:

Over the last few days, several Bollywood celebrities have openly spoken up about the CJP-led student protest. On Monday, after students marched from Jantar Mantar to Parliament and were met with police force, including lathi-charge and tear gas, many celebrities took to social media to condemn the use of force against the protesters. On Wednesday, protests also surfaced in Mumbai. Star Imran Khan was seen participating in the protest march to show his solidarity.

Adding to this, Imran Khan joined the Cockroach Janta Party protests in Mumbai. Imran joins CJP protests A video of Imran marching along with hundreds of students in Mumbai was shared by the X account, Cockroach is Back. Although it was raining, Imran marched along with the crowd with a smile in his face. He was seen fist-bumping with other students and even clicking pictures with eager followers who recognised him in the crowd.

Meanwhile, A day ago, Imran had expressed his discontent over the police violence at Jantar Mantar. He admitted that using lathi-charge against students made no sense, saying they had already been failed by the system. He wrote, “Something broke in me today. Watching as police gassed and battered student protestors… how could I make sense of this? These students who worked hard, only to be failed by the system. These people who asked to be heard, only to be met with silence. These citizens who stood up to show what matters to them… beaten down and disowned by the establishment."

Notably, The star added, “Are these not the children of Mother India? If not them, for whom do we build, and whose future do we claim to protect? Those who demand ‘organisation’ from students protesting education reform, but don’t apply such lofty standards of accountability to established authority; bootlickers, one and all." Instagram post.

As per the latest buzz, A few days ago, he had also expressed support for Sonam Wangchuk ’s hunger strike demanding the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan and wrote, “Standing up for the future @wangchuksworld #ChaloSansad."

In further updates, Many celebrities have come out in support of activist Sonam Wangchuk, who has been on a hunger strike for more than 23 days, and the CJP-led protest. Some of the celebrities include Zeenat Aman, Anurag Kashyap, Shabana Azmi, Ratna Pathak Shah, Abhay Deol, Omi Vaidya, and others.

On top of that, Meanwhile, the Cockroach Janta Party on Wednesday shared thousands of protesters had gathered at the Jantar Mantar, with spokesperson Saurav Das sharing visuals of the crowd swelling at the protest site. In conversation with ANI news agency, CJP spokesperson Ashutosh Ranka claimed “millions more will come” if the government doesn't agree to their demands. Meanwhile, the ministry of home affairs ordered the immediate deployment of 20 additional CRPF companies to Delhi. The CRPF companies are being brought in from West Bengal.

Santanu Das is a Senior Content Producer at Hindustan Times with over 5 years of experience, writing on films, pop culture and movie festivals. He has a keen interest in writing about South Asian independent films and has covered several movie festivals, including Sundance and CPH: Docx. He also brings a sharp perspective to the monthly column called The Fault in Our Stars, where he writes about a recent movie/series and what stops the ‘good’ from becoming ‘great’. A gold medalist from Banaras Hindu University, Santanu completed his postgraduate studies in English from Jadavpur University. He is also a Rotten Tomatoes-certified movie critic. When not watching films or in conversation with celebrities, Santanu can be found reading a book. Some of his favourite films are Aparajito, Ponyo and The Double Life of Veronique. His favourite books include The Corrections, The God of Small Things and A Room of One's Own. Santanu continues to write passionately about films and star culture. He brings a relatable, as well as critically informed, lens to entertainment and culture for a wide audience. Find him on LinkedIn: santanudasfilm Instagram: @santupecha Read More

title: Uorfi Javed says she nearly escaped getting detained by Mumbai Police: ‘Log peaceful protest karna chah rahe hain…’
body: Here's the latest update from the Bollywood world:

Heavy barricading continues at various areas in Delhi, including Parliament House and Rafi Marg, even as the Cockroach Janta Party continues its protest at the Jantar Mantar demanding the resignation of union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan over alleged irregularities in NEET.

Adding to this, Uorfi Javed spoke about how the Mumbai police have detained many who wanted to protest. (Photo: Instagram) A total of six FIRs have been filed so far over the alleged violence and vandalism during the protest march held by the CJP on Monday, at Parliament Street, Connaught Place and other police stations, with police likely to probe a "larger and criminal conspiracy" behind the matter, PTI news agency reported.

Meanwhile, Not just in Delhi, protests have also surfaced in Mumbai. Influencer Uorfi Javed posted on Instagram to share that she and her sisters, Dolly and Asfi, did a fit-check and were on their way to join the protest at Shivaji Nagar but it escalated much differently. The three of them nearly escaped being detained by the Mumbai police. Uorfi posted on Instagram and shared, "Twinning kar ke protest jane wale the, fit-check kar ke. Lekin waha pe to..." She then posted another clip while leaving the protest venue, alleging, "Log peaceful protest karna chah rahe hain. Lekin wo bhi nahi karne de rahe hain police wale (People want to protest peacefully. But even that is not being allowed by the police)."
Notably, View this post on Instagram A post shared by Uorfi (@urf7i)

As per the latest buzz, She added, “Mere samne police wale... log sirf ikattha hi ho rahe the, koi kuch nahi kar raha tha. Log bas waha pe khade ho rahe the... Police unlog ko detain kar ke le ke gayi, bina koi reason ke (People were simply gathering and did not even protest, when they were being detained by the police without any reason).”

In further updates, Star Ayesha Khan took to her Instagram and detailed what happened to her when she arrived to join the protest in Mumbai. She shared that she was dragged without being given any reason and pushed inside a van by force. “I was pulled by 4-5 women. I used all my strength to try not getting into the van but they pulled us in. Poorey van mein hum poochte rahein ki aap ne humein kyu detain kiya hain? Humne galti kya kiya hain? No answer, no accountability, nothing. Kisi ne koi jawab nahi diya aur humein Dadar se door Worli lekar aaye (We asked for answers inside the van that what was our mistake and why we were detained but there was no response or accountability. From Dadar we were taken to Worli),” she shared.

On top of that, This is the third day of protests in Mumbai and several other cities, which were kicked off in New Delhi by the Cockroach Janta Party at Jantar Mantar, moving to Parliament on the first day of the Monsoon session. Sonam Wangchuk, who is on an indefinite hunger strike, was whisked off by the police on Saturday.

Protests were also staged at multiple locations in the city demanding the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan. At Shivaji Park, demonstrations were held near Chaityabhoomi. A protester shared some of them were arrested even before reaching the site.

title: Amaal Mallik claims rival “paid PR” is attacking Awarapan 2 soundtrack: “Save your funds for your next films”
body: Here's the latest update from the Bollywood world:
Amaal Mallik alleges a paid PR campaign is targeting Awarapan 2 after Yeh Awarapan's rollout and urges listeners to judge the song with an open mind.

Adding to this, Taking to social media on July 22, Amaal thanked listeners for the response to the song and urged audiences to give it time before forming an opinion. "Thank you for such a brilliant response to a melody that's the closest to my heart. Every song needs time to breathe, be taken into the system and then reach and live in the heart of the audience," he wrote.

Meanwhile, In the same note, the composer claimed that rival groups were attempting to damage the movie's prospects. "There is a huge paid PR being run by rival elements of the movie industry that don't want the movie or soundtrack to do well," he alleged.

Notably, Amaal added that such efforts would not affect the audience's connection with the music. Addressing those he accused of running the campaign, he wrote, "Please save your funds for your next films, who knows what happens tomorrow. You may waste all your money to attack my song and my movie but if you don't save some of it, you won't be able to save your own films."
As per the latest buzz, The composer also weighed in on the comparisons between Awarapan 2 and the music of the 2007 movie Awarapan , which has long enjoyed cult status among followers.

In further updates, Explaining the background of the original album, Amaal shared the songs in the first movie were adaptations of independent tracks by artists including Mustafa Zahid, Rafaqat Ali Khan and Annie, which were recreated by Pritam for the movie. "So that ends the debate of it being an original album, it's well adapted by Pritam and that's the whole truth," he wrote.
On top of that, He also acknowledged the legacy of the original artists, saying, "We can't do what Mustafa Zahid did, and we don't even wish to touch his legacy and spoil it."

Amaal concluded his note by urging listeners to evaluate the music on its own merits rather than comparing it with the original soundtrack: "Before you judge mine or any musician's work, ek baar aankh bandh karke woh insaan ka gaana dil se suno."

title: Jeetendra recalls buying Pali Hill bungalow for ₹4.25 lakh after Bharat Bhushan's financial woes; land now worth ₹450cr
body: Here's the latest update from the Bollywood world:

Veteran star Jeetendra was among Bollywood's biggest stars in the 1980s. Alongside his flourishing movie career, he also made smart real estate investments and eventually became a well-known name in Mumbai's property market. In a recent interview, the star recalled buying a bungalow in Mumbai's upscale Pali Hill from late star Bharat Bhushan for what he described as a throwaway price.

Adding to this, Jeetendra spoke about the time he bought his first bungalow. Jeetendra bought Bharat Bhushan's bungalow for ₹ 4.25 lakh In conversation with The Realty Drive, Jeetendra opened up that Bharat Bhushan was going through a severe financial crisis and was forced to sell his Pali Hill bungalow for just ₹ 4.25 lakh. According to TOI report, the plot is now valued at around ₹ 450 crore rupees.

Meanwhile, Recalling the purchase, Jeetendra shared, "He was a huge star in those days who had done films like Baiju Bawra. He came to me with the offer to buy his Pali Hill bungalow. When I spoke to my father, he shared, 'Let's also buy a bungalow.' Until then, we had lived a very lower-middle-class life. I used to live in a 20x10 room; eight people stayed together in a chawl. I finally bought the bungalow for ₹ 4.25 lakh. What was even more shocking was that it came with a 3,450 sq ft plot at the same price."

Notably, Jeetendra recalled that soon after shifting into the bungalow, four of his films flopped consecutively. Fearing he might meet the same fate as Bharat Bhushan, who had suffered major financial setbacks, he decided to move out of the house. However, the property later turned out to be one of his most profitable investments.

As per the latest buzz, Years later, when a developer approached him with a redevelopment proposal, Jeetendra readily agreed. He opened up that he collected nearly three times the bungalow's purchase price from the Burma teak wood used in its construction alone.

In further updates, "You won't believe it, but when it was demolished, I received ₹ 11.5 lakh just for the Burma teak used in its construction. I had bought the entire bungalow for ₹ 4.25 lakh and collected ₹ 11.5 lakh from the wood alone," he shared.

On top of that, Veteran star Jeetendra has worked in over 200 films during a career spanning more than six decades. He made his acting debut with Geet Gaya Patharon Ne (1964) and rose to stardom with Farz (1967). Over the years, he became one of Hindi cinema's biggest stars and collected the nickname "Jumping Jack" for his energetic dance moves and lively on-screen performances.

title: Collective Studios partners with Zee 5 to unveil six AI-driven Historyverse originals inspired by Indian mythology and history
body: Here's the latest update from the Bollywood world:
Each of the six titles will premiere across 7 languages – Hindi, Tamil, Telugu, Marathi, Malayalam, Kannada, and Bangla.

Adding to this, By harnessing AI to augment the creative process, Zee 5 is reimagining how stories are created and experienced, while remaining deeply rooted in narratives that resonate with audiences across generations.

Meanwhile, At the heart of this partnership is a six-title slate comprising Garud – Ek Yodha Ki Gatha, Narmada Manasa Sundari- Shiv Parvati Ki Putriyan, Vikram Betal Aur Nar Pishachani, Shiv Sati Ki Ek Anant Prem Kahani, Param Sundari Chandrakanta Ki Amar Prem Katha and Bhakto Ke Bhagwan Tirupati Balaji. Developed by Zee 5 and Collective Studios’ Historyverse, the titles draw inspiration from Indian mythology, folklore, history and timeless literary traditions, transforming beloved stories into immersive AI-powered visual experiences designed for the global audience.

Notably, Each of the six titles will premiere across 7 languages – Hindi, Tamil, Telugu, Marathi, Malayalam, Kannada, and Bangla marking the beginning of a new storytelling universe, bringing India’s timeless narratives to global audiences in innovative and engaging ways.

As per the latest buzz, Tejkarran Singh Bajaj, Business Head - Zee 5 India shared, “The future of entertainment will be defined not just by the stories we tell, but by the technology that powers them. At Zee 5, we see AI and emerging technologies as catalysts for the next era of storytelling, unlocking new creative possibilities, accelerating innovation, and enabling immersive, interactive experiences at scale. Our partnership with Collective Studios’ Historyverse reflects this vision, bringing together cutting-edge technology with India’s rich storytelling heritage to create future-facing entertainment IPs that are built for a global audience. As the entertainment landscape evolves, we remain committed to championing innovation that reimagines how stories are created, experienced, and discovered.”

In further updates, Vijay Subramaniam, Founder & Group CEO, Collective Artists Network, shared: “India's mythology, history and folklore are among the world's richest storytelling traditions, with a timeless ability to inspire, entertain and spark imagination across generations. At HistoryVerse, our ambition is to honour these stories while reimagining how they can be experienced by contemporary audiences.”

On top of that, He added, “Partnering with Zee 5 on this slate is a significant milestone for us. Together, we're bringing culturally rooted narratives to life in fresh, immersive formats that remain true to their essence while opening them up to new audiences in India and beyond.”

As Zee 5 continues to invest in category-defining stories and enduring intellectual properties, this landmark AI-powered slate reinforces its commitment to leading the next wave of entertainment innovation and take Indian stories to the world.

title: Ayesha Khan pushed into van and detained by Mumbai Police, actor says she did not speak a word about protest
body: Here's the latest update from the Bollywood world:

Star Ayesha Khan , who of late made a splash with her special song Shararat in Dhurandhar , has shared that she was detained by the Mumbai police on Wednesday. The star posted on Instagram to share a video statement detailing what happened on the site where she and some of her male and female friends had gathered to show solidarity for the student protests in Mumbai. Ayesha shared that she was dragged without being given any reason and pushed inside a van by force.

Adding to this, Ayesha Khan shared a statement from the police station after being detained from the protest site. Ayesha Khan shares what happened at the site Ayesha shared a video to share her side of the story, where she shared that she was eager to show support towards the student protests that were supposed to take place in Dadar. But when she arrived there at 4 PM, and even before she could say a word or show any kind of slogan there was a police officer who shared that she cannot protest there. Ayesha asked in response, ‘Bata dijiye dusra aur kya raasta hain (Please tell us what is the other option)?’ At this point, before any conversation, the police pulled her brother, friend and then around 15 policemen (10 men and 5 women) told her and her friends that they have to get in the van.
Meanwhile, View this post on Instagram A post shared by Ayesha Khan (@ayeshaakhan_official)

Notably, “I was pulled by 4-5 women. I used all my strength to try not getting into the van but they pulled us in. Poorey van mein hum poochte rahein ki aap ne humein kyu detain kiya hain? Humne galti kya kiya hain? No answer, no accountability, nothing. Kisi ne koi jawab nahi diya aur humein Dadar se door Worli lekar aaye (We asked for answers inside the van that what was our mistake and why we were detained but there was no response or accountability. From Dadar we were taken to Worli).”
As per the latest buzz, She shared that the police at Worli spoke to them respectfully but her question remains the same: Why were they detained on ground? What was the reason?
In further updates, View this post on Instagram A post shared by Ayesha Khan (@ayeshaakhan_official)

On top of that, In a separare post, Ayesha shared the video of her being pushed inside the police van. In the caption, she wrote, "Dragged, detained for standing in peace. Asked questions to all the police people present there on why were we being detained? But no answer whatsoever! Sadak pe khade rehna kabse crime ho Gaya? Today will be the most peaceful sleep of my life. I stood for what was right! Still in the police station asking the same question! Why am I detained? Which law did I break? Forget about one’s democratic right, and protecting it, instead you are harming your own citizens, for what? I see shame in some of their eyes, but they are bound by their duties, they know, we know, what’s right and what’s wrong.
My stand is clear. We lack accountability, EVERYWHERE. I still have hope, we will see a better future."

title: Armaan Malik postpones birthday song release amid Jantar Mantar protests; says, “Things just don’t feel right now”
body: Here's the latest update from the Bollywood world:
The singer extends solidarity with students protesting over the alleged NEET paper leak, days after speaking out against the police crackdown.

Adding to this, Taking to X (formerly Twitter), Armaan shared a video explaining his decision. He shared, “Hey everyone, this next song of mine, ‘Gaya Kaam Se’, I poured my heart into it and it is scheduled to rollout on 22nd of July which is tomorrow, which is also my birthday. But things just don’t feel right now and I feel like I want to wait a little longer and give it to you when the time feels right and when we’re in better circumstances. So yeah, I hope you understand."

Meanwhile, The decision comes two days after videos of the police lathi charge and the use of tear gas against students protesting at Jantar Mantar went widely shared on social media. The incident prompted several members of the movie fraternity to voice their support for the protesters, with Armaan also sharing a statement on the issue.

Notably, Expressing his thoughts in a note, the singer wrote, “I don't claim to have all the answers, and I believe it's important to understand every issue with care before speaking. But there are some values we should never lose sight of. Students deserve fairness. Peaceful protests deserve respect. Empathy should never be conditional, and dialogue should never be met with indifference. Our country and its people deserve to be heard. Always.”

As per the latest buzz, Armaan Malik is among several celebrities who have publicly supported the students demanding accountability over the alleged paper leak. While many Indian celebrities across have shared messages of solidarity on social media, the singer's decision to postpone the rollout of his birthday track marks one of the most significant gestures of support from the entertainment industry amid the ongoing protests.

title: Max, Min and Meowzaki review: Adil Hussain, Mandira Bedi, Nassar make a heartfelt film about 2nd chances a breezy watch
body: Here's the latest update from the Bollywood world:

For a movie that opens with an amicable breakup between a young couple, you would hardly expect it to go on to tackle themes like homosexuality, domestic violence and loneliness, all in one go. Yet, Max, Min and Meowzaki pulls it off, and for the most part, it does so fairly well.

Adding to this, Max, Min and Meowzaki review: The movie is directed by Padmakumar Narasimhamurthy. Max, Min and Meowzaki story Directed by Padmakumar Narsimhamurthy, the story revolves around Mahesh aka Max (Siddharth Menon) and Minara Hussain aka Min (Medha Shankr), who have decided to part ways. Before leaving, Min asks Max to look after their cat, Meowzaki, for a few days, as her new boyfriend, a movie director, will keep her occupied with a shoot. Allergic to cats and still grappling with the loss of his mother, Max agrees reluctantly.

Meanwhile, Meanwhile, his father, businessman Ramesh ( Adil Hussain ), begins visiting therapist Dhaara ( Mandira Bedi ) as he struggles with sleeplessness. Ramesh's father and Max's grandfather, music legend Sridhar (Nassar), is spending his twilight years at an old-age home, where he strikes up a friendship with Jennifer (Nafisa Ali), who is living with Alzheimer's. How these three narrative threads converge forms the rest of the movie.

Notably, The one thing that stands out immediately about this movie is how breezy it feels. While the title may suggest a straightforward human-animal bond story, the first half is instead anchored in an easygoing romance, before gradually shifting gears to explore how adults navigate grief and regret.

As per the latest buzz, The director handles these transitions with a gentle touch, allowing the narrative to evolve organically rather than forcing its emotional beats. The movie also benefits immensely from the presence of seasoned actors such as Adil and Nassar, whose understated performances lend weight to the proceedings. It is this emotional grounding that elevates several key moments, ensuring that Max, Min and Meowzaki quietly touch the heart and keep you invested in its characters.

In further updates, What feels half-baked, however, is the movie's exploration of modern relationships via Max and Min, and his subsequent quest for love. It introduces interesting ideas about changing notions of attachment and moving on, but rarely delves deep enough to make them resonate. What could have been a nuanced commentary on contemporary romance ends up feeling more like a narrative device than a fully realised emotional arc. The inter-religion angle too seems unexplored.
On top of that, Also, far too much time and dialogue are committed to justify the title's 'Meowzaki'. After a point, it wears the viewer down.

Adil Hussain delivers a compelling performance as a man who realises his follies far too late in life, yet possesses the courage to acknowledge them. His portrayal is understated and deeply affecting, adding emotional depth to the movie. His scenes with Mandira Bedi, who plays his therapist, are among the movie's best, unfolding with honesty and quiet vulnerability. Equally memorable are his interactions with Nassar, with the two veteran actors bringing remarkable gravitas. Medha Shankr doesn't get much screen time, but she fares well.

title: Disha Patani's sister Khushboo says she faced lathi charge at CJP protest: ‘Main khud bhag rahi hu, mujhe khud padey he’
body: Here's the latest update from the Bollywood world:

As many as 50,000 protestors took to streets in the national capital on Monday to demand the resignation of education minister Dharmendra Pradhan , according to HT reporters on ground. The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) called for a march from Jantar Mantar, where the protestors have been camped for a month, to the Parliament, attracting a huge response, with thousands descending on the designated protest site on Monday. It was met with several challenges. This included lathi charge and use of tear gas shells by security forces to disperse the crowd marching towards the Parliament.

Adding to this, Khushboo Patani shared pictures from the Cockroach Janta Party march. What Khushboo shared Disha Patani 's sister, Khushboo Patani , posted on Instagram to document her presence at the protest, as she joined the students in incognito mode. She covered her face with a mask, sported a cap and attended the protest at Jantar Mantar. Taking to her Instagram Stories, Khushboo shared how safe the entire space of the protest felt, and gave a shoutout for all the men who attended the protest. Khushboo shared that there were lakhs of people but at no point did women feel unsafe and there were several instances where men put up their hands over their heads and walked so that they would not make any bad touch. Khushboo shared that she is now a fan of the Gen Z and loved their spirit of protests.
Meanwhile, View this post on Instagram A post shared by Major Khushboo Patani(KP) (@khushboo_patani)

Notably, She went on to add, “Lakhon mein students thhe aur law enforcement sirf hazaaron mein tha. Karne ke liye toh koi kuch bhi kar sakta hain I mean aap duniya bhar ke protests dekh lo. Our Gen Z students chose peace. Lathi kha li lekin haath nahi uthaaya… maara nahi kisine. Until jab tak I think sar-war na phad diye inke, tange nahi tod diye. Ladkiyon ko bohot peeta hain. Main khud bhag rahi hoon, mujhe toh khud padey hain. What I am trying to tell you is I have seen this, standing in the corner that side and when it happened, sab bhaagey peeche ke taraf (Students were in lakhs and law enforcement was in hundreds. They could have done anything given the examples of so many protests in the world. But our Gen Z students did not raise their hands, until they were beaten badly. Some got hit on their heads and legs. Girls were beaten badly. I myself was running, I also got some of that. Everyone ran after they saw what was happening).”

As per the latest buzz, The recent student protest led by the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) began as a youth-led movement demanding reforms in India's education system. The protesters have been calling for the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan over alleged irregularities in the education sector, particularly the alleged NEET paper leaks . Social activist Sonam Wangchuk also joined the protest earlier this month and began a hunger strike at Jantar Mantar.

In further updates, On Monday, the protest turned violent as demonstrators marched towards Parliament. According to Delhi Police, protesters displayed aggressive and violent behaviour, refused to disperse despite repeated warnings and violated prohibitory orders in force. The protesters, however, alleged that police personnel used force indiscriminately, resulting in injuries to several demonstrators. Several public figures, including Shabana Azmi , Pratibha Ranta and Prakash Raj , have expressed support for the students. Although the protesters met representatives of the central government on Tuesday, the agitation is continuing.

On top of that, Santanu Das is a Senior Content Producer at Hindustan Times with over 5 years of experience, writing on films, pop culture and movie festivals. He has a keen interest in writing about South Asian independent films and has covered several movie festivals, including Sundance and CPH: Docx. He also brings a sharp perspective to the monthly column called The Fault in Our Stars, where he writes about a recent movie/series and what stops the ‘good’ from becoming ‘great’. A gold medalist from Banaras Hindu University, Santanu completed his postgraduate studies in English from Jadavpur University. He is also a Rotten Tomatoes-certified movie critic. When not watching films or in conversation with celebrities, Santanu can be found reading a book. Some of his favourite films are Aparajito, Ponyo and The Double Life of Veronique. His favourite books include The Corrections, The God of Small Things and A Room of One's Own. Santanu continues to write passionately about films and star culture. title: Swara Bhasker speaks out on additional CRPF deployment amid CJP protest
body: Here's the latest update from the Bollywood world:

Since Monday, when students allegedly faced lathi-charge while marching in Delhi demanding the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan following the NEET paper leak row, many celebrities have come out in support of the protest. Bollywood star Swara Bhasker has been one of the first celebrities to publicly back the student movement. Now, she has shared a new video, calling out the government's reported deployment of additional CRPF companies in Delhi.

Adding to this, Bollywood star Swara Bhasker spoke against government's reported decision to deploy additional security forces in Delhi. 'Indian government has declared war on youth' Swara shared a video in which she expressed her discontent over the government's reported deployment of more security forces in Delhi to deal with students who have been peacefully protesting in the national capital. She shared that these measures indicated that the "Indian government has declared war on the youth of this country."

Meanwhile, She shared in the video, "What is really screwed up about the government response to the Delhi protest is that they have the Delhi police in the full riot gear and on like the battle front, they have RAF (Rapid Action Force), they have 20 additional companies of CRPF coming to Delhi and now it is reported that they are also calling 10 more companies of CRPF from Kashmir. So, apparently students, who are leading a peaceful protest and are fighting for their own future, and have totally legitimate demands, are more dangerous to the Indian government than Pakistan and China. Let's say it s it is: The Indian government has declared war on the youth and children of this country. It is absolutely shameful."

Notably, She captioned the post, "It feels like the Indian Govt. has declared war on the children and youth of our country! Shame!!" According to PTI, Centre has ordered deployment of 20 additional companies of the CRPF in Delhi to strengthen security measures.
As per the latest buzz, View this post on Instagram A post shared by Swara Bhasker (@reallyswara)

In further updates, Meanwhile, Anupam Kher has also spoken on the issue. However, he warned students to remain cautious of political parties using their movement to further their own political agendas. He in a X video, "But I want to say this thing as a big brother to you all, when external parties start entering your protest, especially political parties, who want to register their presence in every protest. Their aim is not to advance your cause, but to fulfil their own vested interests. You should be careful in this case. You might feel in the beginning that the more people join you, your protest will get more powerful, but it is not like that always. Many times, these same people start using your voices; they are just there to serve their political agendas. Later, the cause doesn’t remain with you; they take away the cameras and slogans with them. In this case, you and your protest suffer your truth suffers."

On top of that, For the unversed, activist Sonam Wangchuk had been on a 25-day hunger strike demanding the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan over alleged structural failures and examination irregularities, including the NEET paper leak. The protest was backed by the Cockroach Janata Party (CJP). On Monday, CJP-led student protesters marched from Jantar Mantar to Parliament as part of their 'Chalo Sansad' protest. During the march, the students allegedly faced lathi-charge, and the police also as per reports used tear gas to disperse the crowd.

title: Kiran Kumar: Welcome to the Jungle has given a new lease of life to actors; so sweet of Akshay Kumar to give us credit
body: Here's the latest update from the Bollywood world:

Veteran star Kiran Kumar is elated with the love his recent rollout Welcome to the Jungle is getting. He is touched with lead star Akshay Kumar ’s recent post crediting his and star Farid Jalal’s blessing for the success of the movie.

Adding to this, Star Kiran Kumar and (right) the post by Akshay Kumar crediting the veteran actors' blessing for the success of the movie (Instagram) The star says, “ Welcome to the Jungle ’s success has given a new lease of life to actors. With so many actors around and still your work is noticed and appreciated by people gives a great feeling that my hard work has paid. It’s a major star cast and credit goes to the director Ahmed Khan for ably handling the characters.”
Meanwhile, View this post on Instagram A post shared by Akshay Kumar (@akshaykumar)

Notably, In his post, Akshay has posted: “While I am indebted to every single soul connected to Welcome to the Jungle , I bow down in gratitude to seniors like Farida ji and Kiran Sir. It must be their blessings which is seeing our movie get such unimaginable love from all of you…”

As per the latest buzz, “It’s so sweet of Akshay babu to give us the credit for the movie’s success – don’t know I deserve it but he feels so! 35 years back, I started working with him including the iconic Dhadkan (2000). This shows how big a heart he has,” he says and adds, “Me and Farida ji both came after the interval and our chemistry still worked. I have worked with her in Heena (1991). I feel happy for everybody.
In further updates, View this post on Instagram A post shared by Akshay Kumar (@akshaykumar)
On top of that, When the audience laughs, claps and whistles for you it's the greatest feeling for an star.”

The star says that he is getting a lot of phone calls from producers and directors who would like to work with him. “I have never craved personal glory. For me, my movie should work as everyone gets benefits from it. Thodi bahut tareef se mera kaam chal jayega par meri movie chalni chahiye! I was 18 when I started and it has been 53 years in industry. I too have evolved from that to this era and the biggest learning has been to be simple and subtle which I tried to do in this movie as well.”

title: Vidhu Vinod Chopra Films says 3 Idiots is not re-releasing on September 4
body: Here's the latest update from the Bollywood world:
3 Idiots starred Aamir Khan, R. Madhavan, Sharman Joshi, Kareena Kapoor Khan, Boman Irani and Omi Vaidya.

Adding to this, The official statement read, “With reference to recent media reports claiming that 3 Idiots will be re-rolled out on 4th September, Vidhu Vinod Chopra Films would like to clarify that these reports are false and factually incorrect. We request members of the media and followers to rely solely on official announcements made through Vidhu Vinod Chopra Films’ verified channels. We urge media publications to refrain from publishing or circulating unverified information.”
Meanwhile, The clarification comes after speculation surrounding a possible theatrical re-rollout of 3 Idiots gained traction across media reports and social media platforms.

Notably, Rolled out in 2009 and directed by Rajkumar Hirani, 3 Idiots starred Aamir Khan, R. Madhavan, Sharman Joshi, Kareena Kapoor Khan, Boman Irani and Omi Vaidya. The coming-of-age comedy-drama went on to become one of the biggest blockbusters of its time and continues to enjoy immense popularity among audiences. The movie went onto become the first one in Hindi to surpass not just Rs. 150 crores but also Rs. 200 crores. Its final tally stood at a staggering Rs. 202.95 crores.

title: R. Madhavan to host Jeena Isi Ka Naam Hai 2.0 as iconic talk show returns on Zee 5
body: Here's the latest update from the Bollywood world:
R. Madhavan will host Jeena Isi Ka Naam Hai 2.0 on Zee5 as the iconic talk show returns with conversations featuring notable personalities.

Adding to this, According to the makers, Jeena Isi Ka Naam Hai 2.0 will feature conversations with personalities who have made significant contributions in their respective fields. The new season aims to explore their personal journeys, relationships and defining moments beyond their public achievements.

Meanwhile, Speaking about hosting the show, Madhavan shared, "Today, we have unprecedented access to the lives of people we admire. We know what they do, where they go, and what they share, but we rarely get to know who they truly are. That's what makes Jeena Isi Ka Naam Hai so relevant even today."

Notably, He also reflected on the legacy of the original series and its former host. "The original show, under Farooq Shaikh, created a rare space for honest, deeply human conversations that celebrated the person beyond the public persona. To carry forward a legacy like that is both an honour and a responsibility," he shared.

As per the latest buzz, Madhavan added that the makers hope to retain the essence of the original while making it relevant for today's audience. "As Jeena Isi Ka Naam Hai returns on Zee 5, our endeavour is to preserve the soul of the original while making it resonate with today's audiences. This season, we'll bring together extraordinary personalities and legends who have taken India to the global stage, while uncovering the defining moments, relationships and untold stories that shaped their journeys because beyond every achievement and every headline is a human story waiting to be told, and those are the stories that truly stay with us," he concluded.
In further updates, Jeena Isi Ka Naam Hai 2.0 is slated to premiere soon on Zee 5.
